title: Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis
taxonomy: Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is part of the genus Bolbophyllaria. Further classification places it as a species within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. This species is native to Central America, where it is found in tropical forests and mountainous regions. Within the Orchidaceae family, Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis is closely related to other species in the Bolbophyllaria genus. Taxonomically, it is recognized as Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis (Luer) B.M. Singer, as per current botanical classification.
uses: Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis, also known as the Panama hat orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Central America. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health issues. The roots of the Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis are used in traditional medicine to treat fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Bolbophyllaria pachyrachis.
